channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-19855] e2fsck incorrectly links EA inodes to lost+found
item.description:
e2fsck complains about "Unattached inodes" after running it on a MDT at the CEA:

Shine.Lustre.Actions.Fsck INFO xxxx-MDT0001 b'Pass 4: Checking reference counts'
Shine.Lustre.Actions.Fsck INFO xxxx-MDT0001 b'Unattached inode 238467314'
Shine.Lustre.Actions.Fsck INFO xxxx-MDT0001 b'Connect to /lost+found? yes'
....

These are EA inodes (ea_inode feature, contained trusted.link data):

debugfs> stat <238467339>
Inode: 238467339   Type: regular    Mode:  0600   Flags: 0x200000     <--- EA_INODE_FL
Generation: 1304211708    Version: 0x00000000:00000001
User: 13639   Group: 32063   Project:     0   Size: 4071
File ACL: 0
Links: 1   Blockcount: 8
Fragment:  Address: 0    Number: 0    Size: 0
 ctime: xxxx -- Thu Jan  1 01:00:00 1970
 atime: xxxx -- Wed Nov  5 11:50:37 2188
 mtime: xxxx -- Thu Jan  1 08:52:51 2026
crtime: xxxx -- Thu Jan  1 08:52:51 2026
Size of extra inode fields: 32
BLOCKS:
(0):149628227
TOTAL: 1

But the EA inodes are connected to parents inodes (found via trusted.link data contained in the EA inode):

Inode: 238467186   Type: regular    Mode:  0644   Flags: 0x0
Generation: 642869650    Version: xxxxx
User: 13639   Group: 32063   Project:     0   Size: 0
File ACL: 0
Links: 104   Blockcount: 8
[...]

inode <238467339> linkea: idx=0 ....
....
item.summary: e2fsck incorrectly links EA inodes to lost+found

status.description: The issue is open and ready for the assignee to start work on it.

comments.comment:
"Li Dongyang <dongyangli@ddn.com>" merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/tools/e2fsprogs/+/63815/
Subject: LU-19855 e2fsck: fix merging ea_inode_refs count
Project: tools/e2fsprogs
Branch: master-lustre
Current Patch Set:
Commit: b1e429e88c3c162e78c4c55af33b3ffd4e02c1e3

"Etienne AUJAMES <eaujames@ddn.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/tools/e2fsprogs/+/63815
Subject: LU-19855 e2fsck: fix merging ea_inode_refs count
Project: tools/e2fsprogs
Branch: master-lustre
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 520904eda4f5039b6586f20d34fff0ef84e497b5

Here the full analysis (gdb e2fsck on the e2image MDT backup before the e2fsck):

One thread scan the parent inode and set the ref to 1 in its local context:

Thread 1 "e2fsck" hit Breakpoint 3, e2fsck_pass1_merge_ea_inode_refs (global_ctx=xxxx, thread_ctx=xxxx) at pass1.c:3107
3107                    retval = ea_refcount_store(global_ctx->ea_inode_refs,
(gdb) p ino
$52 = 238467339
(gdb) p global_count
$53 = 0
(gdb) p thread_cout
No symbol "thread_cout" in current context.
(gdb) p thread_count
$54 = 1

Another thread scan the EA inode and set the ref to EA_INODE_FL (via track_ea_inode()) and then e2fsck_pass1_merge_ea_inode_refs() sum the refs:

Thread 1 "e2fsck" hit Breakpoint 3, e2fsck_pass1_merge_ea_inode_refs (global_ctx=xxxx, thread_ctx=xxxx) at pass1.c:3107
3107                    retval = ea_refcount_store(global_ctx->ea_inode_refs,
(gdb) p ino
$56 = 238467339
(gdb) p global_count
$57 = 1
(gdb) p thread_count
$58 = 18446744073709551615
(gdb) p 18446744073709551615 + 1
$59 = 0

Then ea_refcount_store() remove the ref for the ea_inode from rb_tree:

errcode_t ea_refcount_store(ext2_refcount_t refcount, ea_key_t ea_key,
                            ea_value_t ea_value)
{
        struct ea_refcount_el   *el;

        /*                         
         * Get the refcount element
         */                            
        el = get_refcount_el(refcount, ea_key, ea_value ? 1 : 0);
        if (!el)                       
                return ea_value ? EXT2_ET_NO_MEMORY : 0;
        el->ea_value = ea_value;   
        if (el->ea_value == 0) {
                ext2fs_rb_erase(&el->node, &refcount->root);
                ext2fs_free_mem(&el); 
        }
        return 0;
}

In pass4 no ref is found so the inode is attached in lost+found:

void e2fsck_pass4(e2fsck_t ctx)
{
....
                if (ctx->ea_inode_refs) {
                        ea_refcount_fetch(ctx->ea_inode_refs, i, &ea_refs);
                        if (ea_refs) {
                                /*
                                 * Final consolidation of EA inodes. We either
                                 * decide the inode is fine and set link_counted
                                 * to one, or we decide this is actually a
                                 * normal file and clear EA_INODE flag, or
                                 * decide the inode should just be deleted.
                                 */
                                check_ea_inode(ctx, i, &last_ino, inode,
                                               &link_counted, ea_refs);
                        }
                }

                if (link_counted == 0) {
                        if (!buf)
                                buf = e2fsck_allocate_memory(ctx,
                                     fs->blocksize, "bad_inode buffer");
                        if (e2fsck_process_bad_inode(ctx, 0, i, buf))
                                continue;
                        if (disconnect_inode(ctx, i, &last_ino, inode))
...
